title: Coffee on cotton — quick help
summary: Act quickly and start with cool or lukewarm water.
steps: Rinse the stain from the back of the fabric.
Do not use hot water at the beginning.
warnings: Test any cleaner on a hidden area first.

title: Coffee on cotton — full guide
summary: Do not dry the item until the stain is gone.
steps: Rinse from the back with cool or lukewarm water.
Apply a small amount of mild detergent.
Gently rub with your fingers or a soft cloth.
Wash according to the care label.
dontDo: do not dry before removing the stain

title: Old coffee stain — quick help
summary: Soak first and avoid heat.
steps: Soak the stain in lukewarm water.
warnings: Do not iron or tumble dry the stain.

title: Old coffee stain — full guide
summary: Use soaking, detergent and repeat if needed before drying.
steps: Work from the outside toward the center.
dontDo: do not tumble dry before checking
do not use harsh chemicals on delicate fabric

title: Tea stain — quick help
summary: Rinse tea stains with cool water as soon as possible.
steps: Do not rub the stain into the fabric.
warnings: Test cleaner on a hidden area first.

title: Tea stain — full guide
summary: Tea may leave a yellow mark, so rinse and use mild detergent.
steps: Rinse with cool water from the back.
dontDo: do not rub silk or wool strongly

title: Grease — quick help
summary: Absorb excess grease without rubbing it deeper.
steps: Press a paper towel on the stain.

title: Grease — full guide
summary: Use a small amount of dish soap to break down grease.
steps: Absorb excess grease with paper towel.
Apply a small amount of dish soap.

title: Red wine — quick help
summary: Do not rub. Blot first.
steps: Rinse with cool water from the back.

title: Red wine — full guide
summary: Blot and wash gently before drying.
steps: Rinse with cool water from the back.

title: Fresh blood — quick help
summary: Do not use hot water on blood stains.

title: Fresh blood — full guide
summary: Cold water and patience are key.
steps: Rinse from the back with cold water.
Do not dry until the stain is gone.
dontDo: do not dry before removing the stain

title: Old blood stain — quick help
summary: Start with longer soaking in cold water.
warnings: Hot water can set the stain.

title: Old blood stain — full guide
summary: Use cold soaking and mild detergent.
steps: Soak in cold water for 30 minutes.
Change water if it becomes colored.

title: Grass — quick help
summary: Do not rub dry grass pigment into the fabric.

title: Grass — full guide
summary: Soak and use detergent to handle pigment.

title: Mud — quick help
summary: Let mud dry first instead of smearing it.

title: Mud — full guide
summary: Remove dry dirt mechanically, then wash.
dontDo: do not dry if a mark remains

title: Pen ink — quick help
summary: Remove ink carefully to avoid spreading.
steps: Place paper towel under the stain.

title: Pen ink — full guide
summary: Work locally and change the backing often.
steps: Put paper towel under the fabric.
Change the backing as it absorbs ink.
dontDo: do not use harsh cleaner without testing

title: Cosmetics — quick help
summary: Remove excess product without rubbing.
warnings: Be careful with silk and wool.

title: Cosmetics — full guide
summary: Treat makeup as pigment plus grease.
dontDo: do not use bleach without checking label

title: Rust — quick help
summary: Rust can be hard. Do not use random chemicals.
warnings: Consider professional cleaning for delicate fabric.

title: Rust — full guide
summary: Use a rust stain product carefully and test first.
steps: Test rust remover on a hidden area.
dontDo: do not use harsh chemicals on delicate fabric

title: Quick stain help
summary: Start safely: remove excess and check the care label.
warnings: Do not use harsh cleaner without testing.

title: General stain removal guide
summary: When unsure, act gently and avoid heat.
steps: Test cleaner on a hidden area.
Start with cool or lukewarm water.
Do not dry until the stain is gone.
dontDo: do not start with high heat
